enonce.texte: Read the sentences below. For each one, identify the tense of the underlined verb as Present Simple, Past Simple, or Future Simple (will).

questions.texte: Identify the tense of the underlined verb in each sentence.

sousQuestions.texte: She <u>drinks</u> water every morning.
sousQuestions.reponse: Present Simple

sousQuestions.texte: They <u>visited</u> the Limbe beach last year.
sousQuestions.reponse: Past Simple

sousQuestions.texte: I <u>will call</u> you tomorrow after school.
sousQuestions.reponse: Future Simple (will)

sousQuestions.texte: The sun <u>rises</u> in the east.
sousQuestions.reponse: Present Simple

sousQuestions.texte: We <u>ate</u> eru and plantain for dinner.
sousQuestions.reponse: Past Simple

intitule: Exercise 2: Complete the Sentences with the Correct Tense

enonce.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb in parentheses. Use Present Simple, Past Simple, or Future Simple ($will$).

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct verb tense.

sousQuestions.texte: My father always (work) _______ hard at the farm.
sousQuestions.reponse: works

sousQuestions.texte: We (see) _______ a tornado near Foumban last week.
sousQuestions.reponse: saw

sousQuestions.texte: She (not/attend) _______ the meeting next Monday.
sousQuestions.reponse: will not attend / won't attend

sousQuestions.texte: _________ you (buy) _________ the vegetables at the market yesterday?
sousQuestions.reponse: Did ... buy

sousQuestions.texte: If it rains, the ceremony (take) _______ place inside.
sousQuestions.reponse: will take

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences as instructed. Pay attention to time expressions.

questions.texte: Apply the rules of tense transformation.

sousQuestions.texte: Change to Past Simple: 'He always buys fresh fish at the Mfoundi market.'
sousQuestions.reponse: He always bought fresh fish at the Mfoundi market.

sousQuestions.texte: Change to Future Simple: 'The government plants trees to stop deforestation.'
sousQuestions.reponse: The government will plant trees to stop deforestation.

sousQuestions.texte: Change to Present Simple: 'They will protect endangered species next year.'
sousQuestions.reponse: They protect endangered species.

sousQuestions.texte: Change to Negative Past Simple: 'The child drank all the water.'
sousQuestions.reponse: The child did not drink all the water.

enonce.texte: Each sentence contains a mistake related to the simple tenses. Find the mistake and correct it.

questions.texte: Analyze the sentences and correct the tense errors.

sousQuestions.texte: She go to school by bike every day.
sousQuestions.reponse: She goes to school by bike every day.

sousQuestions.texte: They didn't finished their homework yesterday.
sousQuestions.reponse: They didn't finish their homework yesterday.

sousQuestions.texte: He will to call you as soon as he arrives.
sousQuestions.reponse: He will call you as soon as he arrives.

sousQuestions.texte: My mother cook delicious eru last night.
sousQuestions.reponse: My mother cooked delicious eru last night.

intitule: Exercise 5: Write About Your Daily Life (Real-life Application)

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (4-5 sentences) about your typical morning routine. Then, write what you did yesterday morning, and what you will do tomorrow morning. Use the Present Simple, Past Simple, and Future Simple ($will$) correctly.

questions.texte: Create a paragraph using the three simple tenses to describe your routine, a past action, and a future plan.
questions.reponse: Example answer: 'Every day, I wake up at 6 a.m. and help my parents. Yesterday morning, I swept the compound before breakfast. Tomorrow morning, I will review my English lesson before going to school.' (Student's answer will vary but must show correct tense usage.)

enonce.texte: Read the situation below and answer the questions using the correct simple tenses.

questions.texte: Situation: Your village is facing a drought. Last year, the government built a new well. Every day, the community uses water carefully. Next week, a NGO will visit to teach new farming methods. Based on this situation, answer the following:

sousQuestions.texte: Explain what the community does every day (habit).
sousQuestions.reponse: Every day, the community uses water carefully.

sousQuestions.texte: Describe what the government built last year (past fact).
sousQuestions.reponse: Last year, the government built a new well.

sousQuestions.texte: Predict what the NGO will do next week (future action).
sousQuestions.reponse: Next week, a NGO will visit to teach new farming methods.

sousQuestions.texte: Judge the impact: Why is it important to use water carefully during a drought? Write one sentence using 'because' and a simple tense.
sousQuestions.reponse: It is important to use water carefully because the drought affects all the crops and animals. (Accept any logical answer with correct grammar.)
